question.text: After how many years from the date of listing can IDRs be redeemed/converted into underlying shares?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Redemption/Conversion of IDRs into underlying shares is permitted after 1 year from the date of listing. Two-way fungibility is also permitted — DRs can be converted into underlying shares and vice versa, subject to headroom availability. **The Trap:** Students may confuse this with lock-in periods for other instruments like preferential allotment shares (which have different lock-in periods). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series IX covers IDR issuance procedures. Series XII covers listing and conversion mechanisms. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IDR conversion timeline: 1 year post-listing. Headroom = IDRs originally issued minus IDRs outstanding, adjusted for redeemed IDRs. This prevents unlimited conversion.
